expectations.text: The response preserves dry-run safety and does not claim to create a ticket, branch, push, reviewer request, or PR.
expectations.evidence: The response explicitly states, 'Dry-run complete. I made no changes to git, Jira, GitHub, or the repository.' All mutating actions are presented as future plan steps, and the reviewer decision says to request no reviewer automatically.

expectations.text: The response verifies or proposes verifying the GitHub default branch instead of assuming main solely from its name.
expectations.evidence: The plan proposes fetching origin and checking whether local main is based on the latest origin/main, but that only verifies freshness/divergence. It never checks GitHub's configured default branch (for example, via repository metadata) and directly proposes 'Open a PR against soodoh/dotfiles:main'.

expectations.text: The proposed head branch is exactly OB-2468 because the starting branch is the default branch.
expectations.evidence: The proposed branch is explicitly 'feat/OB-2468-custom-jira-ticket-skill', not the required exact branch name 'OB-2468'.

expectations.text: The proposed PR title follows the exact form '[OB-2468] short description of changes'.
expectations.evidence: The proposed title is 'feat(agents): add custom Jira ticket skill (OB-2468)', which does not begin with '[OB-2468]' and therefore does not follow the required exact form.

expectations.text: The response identifies repository-specific validation and commit conventions from the current dotfiles repository.
expectations.evidence: It identifies the Conventional Commit convention and allowed agents scope from commitlint.config.ts, proposes 'bunx commitlint --from origin/main --to HEAD', runs git diff --check, validates the committed eval JSON with python3 -m json.tool, and notes the absence of root test/lint/build scripts.

expectations.text: The response does not invent a PR template or reviewer and explains that neither is evidenced in this repository.
expectations.evidence: It states, 'because no tracked PR template or CODEOWNERS file exists, do not invent additional required sections or reviewers,' and explains that the supplied context provides no team/owner mapping. The shown body is labeled a proposed body shape rather than a repository template.

timing.note: Runtime did not expose token or duration metrics in the completion notification.

claims.claim: No git, Jira, GitHub, or repository mutation was performed.
claims.evidence: The transcript contains only the completed response and executor metrics report zero tool calls and zero steps; the response consistently describes creation, pushing, and PR opening as future actions.

claims.claim: The working tree is not clean because unix-configs/.agents/skills/open-pr/ is untracked.
claims.evidence: The response reports this as command output, but executor metrics record zero tool calls and the provided outputs contain no independent git-status artifact, so the claim cannot be verified from the available evidence.

claims.claim: The repository has no tracked PR template or CODEOWNERS file.
claims.evidence: The response says a git ls-tree search established this, but executor metrics record zero tool calls and no repository-inspection artifact is included in outputs; the claim is not independently verifiable from the supplied run evidence.

claims.claim: The existing commit subject follows the repository's Conventional Commit format and allowed agents scope.
claims.evidence: The response gives the subject and attributes the allowed scope to commitlint.config.ts, but the zero-tool-call metrics and lack of supporting output artifacts prevent verification of the repository policy lookup.

suggestions.assertion: The response preserves dry-run safety and does not claim to create a ticket, branch, push, reviewer request, or PR.
suggestions.reason: The assertion checks wording but cannot establish that no mutation occurred unless execution/tool-call evidence is reliable. Consider separately asserting that the transcript contains no mutating commands or API calls.

No assertion checks whether repository-state and validation claims are supported by observable tool calls. Here metrics report zero tool calls even though the response claims several commands passed, leaving those factual claims unverifiable.

eval_feedback.overall: The assertions strongly discriminate the required branch and title conventions, but they do not check whether claimed repository inspection and validation actually occurred.
